title: mespilus sanguinea var. chlorocarpa
intro: Mespilus sanguinea var. chlorocarpa, also known as the blood haw, is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant's bark, leaves, and fruits possess medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various gastrointestinal and respiratory issues. The blood haw is valued for its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and fighting infections. The plant can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are made by combining the plant's parts with water or a solvent to extract its medicinal compounds.
